decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at its upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since material tuning is maxed out, the next best lever is to increase the geometric cross-section of the spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (within the 1.5x limit) to pull more heat away from the chip center towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader width expands the lateral area for heat conduction, reducing thermal resistance between the chip and the side boundaries held at 25°C.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Increased heat flux towards left and right Dirichlet boundaries
risk_notes: Widening the spreader approaches the design domain width limit (1.2m); future iterations may require vertical adjustments if width saturates.
Geometric changes may require mesh quality verification if aspect ratios become extreme, though current step is conservative.
